evidence: "Early SiFi film that assisted in setting the SiFi standards to today."
"One of the classic modernish westerns since those of the sixties."
"Outlandish, in some cases, the stories in this edition but still seem to hold up."
"The classic 'punch in the gut' story of a crazed criminal who has a serious problem with 'live and let live' philosophy."
"Very nice DVD that will certainly keep the viewer awake, and on the edge of their seat!"
"Excellent movie of corporate 'juggling' at the top of the ladder. All star cast works well together with tight script and direction (Wise.)"
"A modern classic of animation to relate a contemporary philosophy of the time."
"More of the Sherlock Holmes adventures put on the silver screen by the only real Holmes actor, Basil Rathbone."
"Great cast, with a 'sideways' offering by the great Charles Laughton."
"The idea, special effects, and tight script in this bio-thriller works well today as it did back in the mid-sixties."
"This movie has not lost a step to date."
"The essential statement of futility with real danger mixed with the inability to confront it within the bounds of the law."

"It is still refreshing to be scared out of your 'pants' (or dress) with a picture that doesn't have to use gallons of blood, knives, guns, hatchets, or monsters to keep you reeling with fear and anticipation."

summary: The user has a strong preference for sci-fi, westerns, and animation, appreciating both visual spectacle and high production quality. They enjoy fast-paced narratives with emotional depth, often resonating with classic storytelling and notable actors like Basil Rathbone and Charles Laughton.
